outbound_official.notes: Vietnam's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) publishes bilateral visa exemption agreements at the source URL above. The page is structured primarily as Vietnam's bilateral exemption list (covering both inbound and outbound directions under each bilateral agreement). The outbound ordinary passport (hộ chiếu phổ thông) exemptions extracted here are those explicitly confirmed for Vietnamese ordinary passport holders: all 10 ASEAN members (ASEAN Free Travel for ordinary passports under bilateral pacts) plus Mongolia, Kyrgyzstan, Belarus (bilateral agreement effective 30 Jan 2025, 30 days/entry, max 90 days/year), Cuba, Chile, and Panama. Vietnam does not publish a standalone government-facing outbound visa-free list page equivalent to India's MEA VFF page; this MOFA bilateral page is the authoritative origin-side source. Visa-on-arrival and e-visa availability for Vietnamese citizens is determined by each destination country and is not published on this MOFA page.

Allows up to 30 days per entry with a total stay not exceeding 90 days within any 12-month period. Belarus was NOT included in Vietnam's unilateral 45-day exemption under Resolution 44/NQ-CP (March 2025); the bilateral agreement is the operative instrument.

e_visa.notes: As of August 2023, Vietnam e-visa is open to all countries and territories. Single-entry USD 25, multiple-entry USD 50 (fees confirmed unchanged as of Circular 28/2026/TT-BTC effective April 1, 2026). Valid 90 days from grant date, single or multiple entry. Processing 3 working days. Valid at 13 international airports, 16 land borders, 13 sea ports. Apply at evisa.gov.vn (main portal as of Nov 2024) or evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn.

visa_policy.notes: Vietnam's e-visa is open to all nationalities (90 days, single or multiple entry, USD 25 / USD 50). Unilateral visa exemptions cover 24 countries as of August 2025: Resolution 44/NQ-CP (March 2025) added 12 countries for 45 days all-purposes; Resolution 229/NQ-CP (August 2025) added 12 more European countries for 45 days for tourism purposes only, superseding the earlier Resolution 11/NQ-CP (March-August 2025) which covered only Poland, Czech Republic and Switzerland and required licensed tour operator booking. Bilateral ordinary passport exemptions cover ~15 additional countries (ASEAN members plus Belarus, Kyrgyzstan, Cuba, Chile, Panama). Phu Quoc Island has universal 30-day visa-free exemption for all nationalities for island-only stays; pre-arrival declaration (PAI) mandatory at Phu Quoc airport since June 1, 2026 via prearrival.immigration.gov.vn.

visa_types.name: E-Visa (Electronic Visa)
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of all countries. Single or multiple-entry. Up to 90 days stay, 90-day validity. Applied online at official Vietnamese Immigration Department portal. Accepted at all international entry points (airports, land borders, seaports). USD 25 fee paid online.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival (with Pre-Approved Letter)
visa_types.notes: Only valid at international airports (not land/sea borders). Requires pre-approved letter from Vietnamese Immigration Department (obtained through licensed travel agent or online service — not an official government portal for this specific service). USD 25 stamping fee paid on arrival + service fee for approval letter (varies, typically USD 20-30). Effectively replaced by the e-Visa system for most travelers.

visa_types.name: Work Permit / Business Visa (DN/DL Visa + Work Permit)
visa_types.notes: Foreign workers require both a DN/DL business visa (or DN/LV visa for investors) AND a work permit (Giấy phép lao động) issued by the Ministry of Labour, Invalids and Social Affairs (MOLISA). Work permit valid up to 2 years, renewable. Business visa applied at Vietnamese embassy or via e-visa system. Work permit requires employer sponsorship, criminal background check, health certificate, professional qualification certificates.

credential.subtype: APEC Business Travel Card (ABTC) with 'VNM' on reverse

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must possess a valid APEC Business Travel Card (ABTC) with 'VNM' printed on the back of the card indicating Vietnam pre-clearance. Entry for business trips only. Card issued to citizens of APEC member economies (Australia, Brunei, Chile, China, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Japan, South Korea, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Papua New Guinea, Peru, Philippines, Russia, Singapore, Taiwan, Thailand). USA and Canada are transitional members and do not offer reciprocal entry arrangements. Governed by Law on Entry, Exit, Transit and Residence of Foreigners in Vietnam (Law 47/2014/QH13). Official ABTC search tool at evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn/abtc-search.

Bilateral visa suppression/waiver agreements between Vietnam and each listed country for holders of diplomatic, official, and/or service passports. 'D' notation for some countries (Czech Republic, Estonia, Israel, Malta, Morocco, Poland, Slovakia, Switzerland, Tunisia) indicates diplomatic passport only — see individual bilateral treaties. Stay period of 90 days per bilateral agreements. Source: Vietnam Government Portal official visa exemption page (listing 54+ bilateral agreements) and Law on Entry, Exit, Transit and Residence of Foreigners No. 47/2014/QH13.

Bilateral visa waiver agreements between Vietnam and each listed country for diplomatic, official and service passport holders. 30-day stay. Source: Vietnam Government Portal official visa exemption page (vietnam.gov.vn).

Bilateral visa waiver agreement (agreements dated 19 February 1997, 16 February 1999, 1 April 2000) between Vietnam and Philippines for diplomatic and official passport holders. 21-day stay. Source: Vietnam Government Portal official visa exemption page.

Bilateral visa waiver agreement (dated 1 November 1997) between Vietnam and Brunei for diplomatic and official passport holders. 14-day stay. Source: Vietnam Government Portal official visa exemption page.

Bilateral visa exemption agreements between Vietnam and each listed country for diplomatic and/or official passport holders. Stay duration varies by bilateral agreement and not individually specified in the official source reviewed. Cuba also includes Seaman's passports and ordinary passports for official missions. Brazil agreement dated 12 February 2005 covers diplomatic passports only. Source: Vietnam Government Portal official visa exemption page.

cbi.notes: Vietnam does not operate a formal citizenship-by-investment program. The amended Law on Vietnamese Nationality (effective July 1 2025, Decree 191/2025/ND-CP) provides flexible naturalization for investors and entrepreneurs who make documented contributions to Vietnam's socio-economic development, but there are no published minimum investment thresholds and it is not a structured CBI program. Dual citizenship is permitted under the 2025 nationality law amendments. A proposed 'Golden Visa' 10-year residence program was announced in 2025 but had no enacted legislation as of June 2026.

programs.name: Investor Visa (DT Visa) - Temporary Residence
programs.notes: DT visa is governed by Law No. 47/2014/QH13 (Law on Entry, Exit, Transit and Residence of Foreigners). Four tiers: DT1 (>=100 billion VND or priority sectors, up to 5-year visa, 10-year TRC), DT2 (50-100 billion VND or encouraged sectors, up to 5-year visa, 5-year TRC), DT3 (3-50 billion VND, 3-year visa, 3-year TRC), DT4 (<3 billion VND, 1-year visa). Min_amount shown is DT3 lower threshold (3 billion VND). Permanent residence is possible after qualifying period for scientists/experts with ministerial recommendation, or foreigners sponsored by Vietnamese family after 3+ continuous years. Processing ~5 working days.

programs.name: Special Visa Exemption Card (SVEC) for Highly Skilled Professionals
programs.eligibility: Top-tier foreign talent including: scholars, scientists, university professors, digital technology experts with prestigious international awards; corporate executives from world's top 100 companies by market capitalization; cultural/artistic/sports figures with recognized global influence; elite athletes ranked global top 100; investors and honorary consuls. Must be nominated/invited by a Vietnamese government body or approved institution. Individual self-applications are not accepted.
programs.notes: Established under Decree 221/2025/ND-CP issued August 8, 2025. Card valid up to 5 years, multiple-entry, each stay up to 90 days. Available in physical and digital formats. Not open to freelancers or typical digital nomads.

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Source URLs for Resolution 44/NQ-CP (12-country) entries updated from english.luatvietnam.vn (unofficial) to vnembassy-bruxelles.mofa.gov.vn (official MOFA embassy subdomain), source_official changed from false to true.

CORRECTED: Belarus entry notes updated: confirmed that Belarus was NOT included in Resolution 44/NQ-CP (unilateral 45-day list); instead Belarus has a bilateral ordinary passport agreement (in force January 30, 2025) providing 30 days per entry with max 90 days total per 12-month period. The 30-day max_stay_days in the record is confirmed correct.

The prior Resolution 11/NQ-CP (January 15, 2025, effective March 1 – August 14, 2025) which required licensed tour operator booking for Poland, Czech Republic, and Switzerland has been superseded by Resolution 229 which does not carry the tour operator requirement.
CORRECTED: GBR (UK) entry note updated to mention British National Overseas (BNO) passport holders must obtain a visa.
CORRECTED: E-visa notes updated to confirm fees unchanged under Circular 28/2026/TT-BTC (effective April 1, 2026) and added evisa.gov.vn as the primary portal (since Nov 2024).
CORRECTED: Phu Quoc entry updated to confirm PAI pre-arrival declaration via prearrival.immigration.gov.vn is mandatory from June 1, 2026 and generate a QR code.

CONFIRMED: Resolution 44/NQ-CP covers 12 countries for ALL entry purposes (not just tourism) — correctly recorded.
CONFIRMED: Resolution 229/NQ-CP covers 12 European countries for tourism purposes only — correctly recorded.
CONFIRMED: E-visa open to all nationalities, 90 days, USD 25 single / USD 50 multiple — correctly recorded.
CONFIRMED: DT visa investment thresholds (DT1 >= 100 billion VND, DT2 50-100 billion, DT3 3-50 billion, DT4 < 3 billion) — correctly recorded.

gaps: Kazakhstan ordinary passport visa exemption: listed on Vietnam National Tourism Authority site but stay duration and existence not confirmed from official government source (mofa.gov.vn or xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn). Removed from visa_free list pending verification.

Full list of countries with diplomatic/official/service passport exemptions: individual stay durations for all countries in the full diplomatic agreement list not independently verified against individual bilateral treaties.
Visa on arrival: Current eligibility nationality list and fees not confirmed from direct official source; largely superseded by e-visa.
Exact investment threshold amounts in USD for DT visa tiers not confirmed from official source (VND amounts used).
Proposed 10-year Golden Visa residence program announced May 2025 has no enacted legislation or official application portal as of June 2026.
evisa.gov.vn (main portal since Nov 2024) could not be fetched due to SSL certificate error; data sourced from evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn which remains operational.

APEC Business Travel Card (ABTC) 60-day exemption: codified in Law 47/2014/QH13 and confirmed by APEC.org official press release (2005) and immigration portal ABTC search tool; however, the exact current text of the relevant article in Law 47/2014/QH13 was not independently verified from the primary legislation text.

Resolution 229/NQ-CP tourism-purpose restriction: The resolution text does not appear to require licensed tour operator booking (unlike Resolution 11/NQ-CP which did). This is confirmed by the Vietnam Law Magazine article about Resolution 229. However, if immigration officials apply a narrower interpretation on arrival, this could affect travelers — this ambiguity is unresolved in available official sources.
Phu Quoc Island visa exemption: Source URL updated to visasnews.com (unofficial) as the official prearrival.immigration.gov.vn portal could not be directly scraped.

sources_checked.description: Vietnam Ministry of Foreign Affairs - Vietnam's Visa Exemption List
Embassy of Vietnam in Belgium (MOFA subdomain) - Resolution 44/NQ-CP visa exemption for 12 countries (all purposes, 45 days)
Embassy of Vietnam in Japan - Resolution 229/NQ-CP visa exemption for 12 European countries (tourism purposes, 45 days, Aug 15 2025 – Aug 14 2028)
Vietnam Government Portal - Visa exemption page listing 54+ bilateral agreements for diplomatic/official passport holders
Vietnam Immigration Department (Cuc Quan Ly Xuat Nhap Canh) - official immigration portal
Vietnam National Portal on Immigration - E-visa application page (USD 25 single / USD 50 multiple, 3 working days, 90 days stay)
Vietnam Immigration Department - ABTC Search tool confirming official recognition of APEC Business Travel Card program (60-day visa-free for business)
Vietnam National Electronic Visa system (main portal as of Nov 2024) - could not scrape due to SSL error
Vietnam Pre-Arrival Information (PAI) System - mandatory for Phu Quoc airport arrivals from June 1, 2026
KPMG Flash Alert 2025-081 - Analysis of Vietnam's new immigration regulations and visa exemption policies (March 2025). Confirmed Belarus bilateral agreement details and Resolution 11/NQ-CP scope.
VisasNews - Vietnam pre-arrival declaration mandatory for Phu Quoc from June 1, 2026
Vietnam Law Magazine - Decree 221/2025/ND-CP Special Visa Exemption Card for highly skilled foreigners
Vietnam Law Magazine - Resolution 229/NQ-CP details, confirmed tourism purpose restriction without tour operator booking requirement
Embassy of Vietnam in USA - visa exemption page. Contains information on Vietnamese diaspora certificate of visa exemption only; no credential-based third-country visa rules found.
